notes.content: UC Santa Cruz students may complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and credit limits. Minors are recorded on the official transcript but do not appear on the diploma. Pursuing a minor is optional and should align with a student’s academic interests and career goals. Students do not apply for minors as part of their UC Santa Cruz admission application. Transfer students interested in a minor should reach out to the department that offers it for advising during their first quarter of enrollment.
notes.contentHtml:
UC Santa Cruz students may complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and credit limits. Minors are recorded on the official transcript but do not appear on the diploma. Pursuing a minor is optional and should align with a student’s academic interests and career goals.


Students do not apply for minors as part of their UC Santa Cruz admission application. Transfer students interested in a minor should reach out to the department that offers it for advising during their first quarter of enrollment.

notes.content: It is strongly recommended that community college students complete all General Chemistry coursework for their intended major at one institution prior to transfer to UC Santa Cruz. Additionally, not all community colleges offer a full year of Organic Chemistry that matches UCSC's Organic Chemistry sequence. As a result, students may need to take some Organic Chemistry courses at UC Santa Cruz, even if they’ve completed a full year of introductory Organic Chemistry at a community college. Please visit the Chemistry & Biochemistry website to learn more about this minor. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Chemistry Minor Program Statement
notes.contentHtml:
It is strongly recommended that community college students complete all General Chemistry coursework for their intended major at one institution prior to transfer to UC Santa Cruz. Additionally, not all community colleges offer a full year of Organic Chemistry that matches UCSC's Organic Chemistry sequence. As a result, students may need to take some Organic Chemistry courses at UC Santa Cruz, even if they’ve completed a full year of introductory Organic Chemistry at a community college.



Please visit the Chemistry & Biochemistry website to learn more about this minor.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Chemistry Minor Program Statement
